chunks:
# ==============================================================================
# IVD Recipe: Role-Based Agent Pattern (Experimental)
# ==============================================================================
# Pattern for AI agents that switch between behavioral modes depending on
# context — each mode has different constraints, tool subsets, and verification.
#
# Status: Experimental
# Version: 1.0
# Created: 2026-02-09
# ==============================================================================

recipe:
  name: "Role-Based Agent Pattern"
  version: "1.0"
  category: "agent_patterns"
  status: "experimental"
  created: "2026-02-09"

  description: |
    Pattern for AI agents that operate in multiple behavioral modes (roles)
    depending on context. Each role defines:
    - What the agent does in this mode (description)
    - When this role activates (trigger/context)
    - What constraints apply (guardrails specific to this role)
    - Which tools are available (subset of the agent's interface)
    - How to verify the agent behaved correctly in this role (tests)

    This is distinct from the coordinator-intent-propagation pattern:
    - Coordinator pattern = MULTIPLE agents, each with its own intent
    - Role-based pattern  = ONE agent, MULTIPLE behavioral modes

    Roles complement the interface section:
    - interface = WHAT the agent can do (tools, parameters, returns)
    - roles     = HOW the agent behaves in different contexts

  problem_solved: |
    AI agents often switch behavioral modes (implementer, reviewer, architect,
    debugger, teacher) but these modes live only in system prompts or code.
    Without roles in intent:
    - Behavioral expectations are implicit, not verifiable
    - Coordinators can't understand an agent's available modes
    - Rewriting the agent loses the behavioral profiles
    - Users don't know what constraints apply in each mode

  applies_to:
    - "AI coding agents with multiple modes (agent, plan, debug, ask)"
    - "Customer support agents switching between helper, escalator, closer roles"
    - "Development agents that review, implement, and architect"
    - "Educational agents that teach, quiz, and mentor"
    - "Any single agent with 2+ distinct behavioral modes"

  when_to_use:
    - "Agent has 2+ behavioral modes with different constraints"
    - "Each mode restricts or changes which tools are available"
    - "Coordinator needs to understand agent's behavioral modes for routing"
    - "Users need clarity on what the agent will and won't do in each mode"
    - "Role constraints need to be verifiable (not just prompt-based)"

  when_NOT_to_use:
    - "Agent has a single mode (just use interface section)"
    - "Behavioral differences are trivial (tone or prompt only, no constraint changes)"
    - "Each role is a separate agent (use coordinator-intent-propagation recipe)"
    - "Module is not an agent (APIs, CLIs, services don't have roles)"

# ==============================================================================
# TEMPLATE SECTIONS
# ==============================================================================

intent_template:
  summary: "[Agent name] with [N] behavior

de"
    - "Role constraints need to be verifiable (not just prompt-based)"

  when_NOT_to_use:
    - "Agent has a single mode (just use interface section)"
    - "Behavioral differences are trivial (tone or prompt only, no constraint changes)"
    - "Each role is a separate agent (use coordinator-intent-propagation recipe)"
    - "Module is not an agent (APIs, CLIs, services don't have roles)"

# ==============================================================================
# TEMPLATE SECTIONS
# ==============================================================================

intent_template:
  summary: "[Agent name] with [N] behavioral modes for [use case]"
  goal: "Provide [capability] with context-appropriate behavior — switching between [roles] based on [trigger]"
  success_metric: "Role constraints verified in [N]% of interactions; no constraint violations in production"
  stakeholders:
    - "[Primary consumer of the agent]"
    - "[Coordinator or system that routes to this agent]"

interface_template:
  type: "agent"
  tools:
    - name: "[tool_1]"
      description: "Available in all roles"
      parameters: []
      returns: "[return type]"
      test: "tests/test_tool_1.py"

    - name: "[tool_2]"
      description: "Available only in specific roles"
      parameters: []
      returns: "[return type]"
      test: "tests/test_tool_2.py"

roles_template:
  default: "[default_role]"

  switching:
    mechanism: "[user_directed | context_inferred | explicit_command]"
    description: "[How the agent transitions between roles — e.g., user selects, agent infers from request]"

  definitions:
    - name: "[role_1]"
      description: "[One-line: primary behavioral mode]"
      when: "[What triggers this role — user request type, explicit command, context]"
      constraints:
        - "[Constraint 1: what this role MUST do]"
        - "[Constraint 2: what this role MUST NOT do]"
      tools: ["all"]
      verification: "tests/test_role_[role_1].py"

    - name: "[role_2]"
      description: "[One-line: secondary behavioral mode]"
      when: "[Trigger for this role]"
      constraints:
        - "[Constraint 1: e.g., read-only]"
        - "[Constraint 2: e.g., must cite sources]"
      tools: ["[subset of interface tools]"]
      verification: "tests/test_role_[role_2].py"

constraints_template:
  - name: "role_constraint_enforcement"
    requirement: "Agent must operate within the active role's constraints at all times"
    test: "tests/test_role_constraints.py::test_role_boundaries"
    consequence_if_violated: "[What breaks — e.g., reviewer modifies files, implementer skips verification]"

  - name: "role_transition_valid"
    requirement: "Role transitions follow the declared switching mechanism"
    test: "tests/test_role_constraints.py::test_valid_transitions"
    consequence_if_violated: "Agent switches roles unexpectedly, breaking user expectations"

  - name: "default_role_applied"
    requirement: "Agent starts in the declared default role when no explicit role is requested"
    test: "tests/test_role_constraints.py::test_default_role"
    consequence_if_violated: "Agent behavior unpredictable on first interaction"

rationale_templ

"[What breaks — e.g., reviewer modifies files, implementer skips verification]"

  - name: "role_transition_valid"
    requirement: "Role transitions follow the declared switching mechanism"
    test: "tests/test_role_constraints.py::test_valid_transitions"
    consequence_if_violated: "Agent switches roles unexpectedly, breaking user expectations"

  - name: "default_role_applied"
    requirement: "Agent starts in the declared default role when no explicit role is requested"
    test: "tests/test_role_constraints.py::test_default_role"
    consequence_if_violated: "Agent behavior unpredictable on first interaction"

rationale_template:
  decision: "[Why these roles, why these constraints, why this switching mechanism]"
  reasoning: |
    Roles were defined based on:
    - [Observed usage patterns — when do users need different behaviors?]
    - [Constraint requirements — which modes need guardrails?]
    - [Tool safety — which tools should be restricted in which contexts?]
  evidence: "[Link to usage data, user feedback, or design docs]"
  date: "[YYYY-MM-DD]"
  stakeholder: "[Who approved the role definitions]"

risks_template:
  - condition: "Agent violates role constraints"
    action: "Log violation, alert, and consider reverting to default role"
    monitor: "Role constraint test suite + production logging"
    severity: "high"

  - condition: "Role switching mechanism doesn't match user expectations"
    action: "Review switching mechanism; consider adding explicit command support"
    monitor: "User feedback, support tickets about unexpected behavior"
    severity: "medium"

  - condition: "Roles become too many (>5), adding complexity without value"
    action: "Consolidate roles with similar constraints; remove unused roles"
    monitor: "Role usage analytics"
    severity: "low"

# ==============================================================================
# REAL-WORLD EXAMPLES
# ==============================================================================

examples:
  - name: "AI Coding Agent (Cursor-style)"
    description: "Coding assistant with agent, plan, review, and debug modes"
    roles:
      default: "agent"
      switching:
        mechanism: "user_directed"
        description: "User selects mode explicitly via command or the system infers from request type"
      definitions:
        - name: "agent"
          description: "Full implementation mode — writes code, creates files, runs commands"
          when: "User requests building, fixing, creating, or implementing"
          constraints:
            - "Must read relevant files before editing"
            - "Must verify constraints after implementation"
            - "Must check for linter errors after edits"
          tools: ["all"]
          verification: "tests/test_role_agent.py"

        - name: "plan"
          description: "Read-only planning mode — designs approach, evaluates tradeoffs"
          when: "Task is large, ambiguous, or has meaningful tradeoffs"
          constraints:
            - "Read-only: no file modifications, no commands"
            - "Must present alternatives with tradeoffs"
            - "Must produce actionable plan before switching

s:
            - "Must read relevant files before editing"
            - "Must verify constraints after implementation"
            - "Must check for linter errors after edits"
          tools: ["all"]
          verification: "tests/test_role_agent.py"

        - name: "plan"
          description: "Read-only planning mode — designs approach, evaluates tradeoffs"
          when: "Task is large, ambiguous, or has meaningful tradeoffs"
          constraints:
            - "Read-only: no file modifications, no commands"
            - "Must present alternatives with tradeoffs"
            - "Must produce actionable plan before switching to agent mode"
          tools: ["read", "search", "semantic_search"]
          verification: "tests/test_role_plan.py"

        - name: "reviewer"
          description: "Code review mode — finds issues, suggests improvements"
          when: "User requests review, audit, or code check"
          constraints:
            - "Read-only: no file modifications"
            - "Must cite specific file paths and line numbers"
            - "Must check alignment against intent artifact if one exists"
          tools: ["read", "search", "validate"]
          verification: "tests/test_role_reviewer.py"

        - name: "debug"
          description: "Systematic debugging mode — investigates bugs with evidence"
          when: "User reports a bug, error, or unexpected behavior"
          constraints:
            - "Must gather runtime evidence before proposing fixes"
            - "Must form and test hypotheses"
            - "Must verify fix resolves the issue"
          tools: ["all"]
          verification: "tests/test_role_debug.py"

  - name: "Customer Support Agent"
    description: "Support agent that handles, escalates, or closes tickets"
    roles:
      default: "helper"
      switching:
        mechanism: "context_inferred"
        description: "Agent infers role from ticket content, customer sentiment, and issue complexity"
      definitions:
        - name: "helper"
          description: "First-line support — answers questions, provides solutions"
          when: "Standard support request within agent's knowledge base"
          constraints:
            - "Must search knowledge base before responding"
            - "Must not make promises about timelines or refunds"
          tools: ["search_kb", "create_ticket", "send_response"]
          verification: "tests/test_role_helper.py"

        - name: "escalator"
          description: "Escalation mode — routes to human agent with context"
          when: "Issue exceeds agent capability, customer is frustrated, or policy requires human"
          constraints:
            - "Must summarize issue and attempted solutions before escalating"
            - "Must not attempt to resolve issues beyond capability"
          tools: ["search_kb", "escalate_ticket", "send_response"]
          verification: "tests/test_role_escalator.py"

# ==============================================================================
# IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE
# ==============================================================================

implementation_pattern:
  directory_structure: |
    a

xt"
          when: "Issue exceeds agent capability, customer is frustrated, or policy requires human"
          constraints:
            - "Must summarize issue and attempted solutions before escalating"
            - "Must not attempt to resolve issues beyond capability"
          tools: ["search_kb", "escalate_ticket", "send_response"]
          verification: "tests/test_role_escalator.py"

# ==============================================================================
# IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE
# ==============================================================================

implementation_pattern:
  directory_structure: |
    agent/{agent_name}/
    ├── {agent_name}_intent.yaml     # Module intent (with interface + roles)
    ├── agent.py                     # Agent implementation
    ├── roles/                       # Role-specific logic (optional)
    │   ├── implementer.py
    │   ├── reviewer.py
    │   └── ...
    ├── intents/                     # Task-level intents (if needed)
    │   └── {function}_intent.yaml
    └── tests/
        ├── test_role_implementer.py
        ├── test_role_reviewer.py
        └── test_role_constraints.py

  key_implementation_decisions:
    - decision: "How to enforce role constraints at runtime"
      options:
        - "Middleware/decorator that checks active role before tool execution"
        - "Role-aware tool registry that filters available tools"
        - "Pre/post hooks that validate constraints"
      recommendation: "Role-aware tool registry — cleanest separation, easiest to test"

    - decision: "How to store active role state"
      options:
        - "Session/conversation level (persists across turns)"
        - "Per-request (resets each turn)"
        - "Explicit command (user switches, persists until changed)"
      recommendation: "Depends on switching.mechanism — match the declared mechanism"

# ==============================================================================
# BEST PRACTICES
# ==============================================================================

best_practices:
  - "Start with 2-3 roles; add more only when distinct constraints emerge"
  - "Each role must have at least one constraint that differs from other roles"
  - "The default role should be the safest or most common mode"
  - "Role constraints should be testable, not aspirational"
  - "Link tools to interface section — roles restrict, they don't define new tools"
  - "Document switching mechanism clearly — users must know how to change roles"

common_pitfalls:
  - pitfall: "Too many roles with overlapping constraints"
    solution: "Consolidate roles that differ in only tone or prompt, not constraints"
  - pitfall: "Roles defined but not enforced at runtime"
    solution: "Add role constraint tests that verify behavior per mode"
  - pitfall: "Using roles when separate agents would be cleaner"
    solution: "If roles never share tools or context, use coordinator pattern instead"
  - pitfall: "Forgetting to update roles when interface changes"
    solution: "Roles reference interface tools — run ivd_validate to catch drift"

# ==============================================================================
# V

many roles with overlapping constraints"
    solution: "Consolidate roles that differ in only tone or prompt, not constraints"
  - pitfall: "Roles defined but not enforced at runtime"
    solution: "Add role constraint tests that verify behavior per mode"
  - pitfall: "Using roles when separate agents would be cleaner"
    solution: "If roles never share tools or context, use coordinator pattern instead"
  - pitfall: "Forgetting to update roles when interface changes"
    solution: "Roles reference interface tools — run ivd_validate to catch drift"

# ==============================================================================
# VERIFICATION GUIDANCE
# ==============================================================================

verification_pattern:
  role_constraint_tests: |
    For each role, write tests that verify:
    1. Only allowed tools are accessible in this role
    2. Role-specific constraints are enforced
    3. Default role is applied when no role is specified
    4. Role transitions follow the declared mechanism

  example_test: |
    def test_reviewer_is_read_only():
        agent = create_agent(role="reviewer")
        # Reviewer should NOT be able to write files
        result = agent.call("write_file", path="test.py", content="...")
        assert result.denied, "Reviewer must be read-only"

    def test_default_role_is_implementer():
        agent = create_agent()  # No role specified
        assert agent.active_role == "implementer"

# ==============================================================================
# CHANGELOG
# ==============================================================================

changelog:
  - version: "1.0"
    date: "2026-02-09"
    change: "Initial recipe for role-based agent pattern"
    reason: "AI agents with context-dependent behavior had no reusable IVD pattern"
